reasoning: The Lean statement captures Rudin's inequality limsup(aₙ+bₙ) ≤ limsup aₙ + limsup bₙ with the correct "not ∞−∞" side condition. In EReal, the indeterminate sum "∞ − ∞" arises precisely when one limsup equals ⊤ and the other equals ⊥. The hypothesis h1 (limsup a ≠ ⊥ ∨ limsup b ≠ ⊤) rules out (⊥, ⊤), and h2 (limsup a ≠ ⊤ ∨ limsup b ≠ ⊥) rules out (⊤, ⊥); their conjunction is logically equivalent to "the sum on the right is not of the form ∞−∞". The conclusion correctly coerces the real-valued sequences into EReal and states the inequality as limsup of the pointwise sum ≤ sum of limsups, matching the standard interpretation of limsup of a real sequence in the extended reals used throughout Rudin.
